hard.next_step: Continue engaging key stakeholders and decision-makers while scheduling a solution demo and proposal review to validate requirements, align on scope. Additionally, Luke met Neetu Tickoo, Category Manager – Logistics, at ProcureCon 2026, and she expressed interest in a Zycus demo. Recently connected with Peter Rodwell and he mentioned that things are moving a little slow at their end and hence have asked to be connected during the winter period (tentatively November). Further updates to be shared later once received from Peter.
hard.ais_why: Boral has been actively investing in digital transformation to improve operational efficiency, supply chain resilience, and data-driven decision-making across its construction materials business. Procurement in manufacturing and construction is well-positioned to benefit from AI through supplier collaboration, strategic sourcing, contract lifecycle management, spend analytics, procurement intake, and risk management. Given the scale and complexity of Boral's supplier network and indirect/direct procurement operations, AI-powered Source-to-Pay (S2P) solutions can deliver significant value by automating routine tasks, improving sourcing decisions, and enhancing compliance and visibility. This makes Boral a strong candidate for AI-enabled procurement transformation.

north_star_verdict.headline: Boral is a strong-fit ($260k, AIS 9) S2C deal, but Peter Rodwell has parked it to November 2026 citing slow internal progress, and with no champion, no economic buyer, and no forcing function the pause risks becoming indefinite.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 25 Mar 2026 in Initial Interest, advanced to Qualified only 22 Jun 2026 (14 days in Qualified as of 6 Jul; ~90 days sat in Initial Interest first). Placeholder close date reset from 25 Mar 2026 to 25 Mar 2027 on 26 Jun 2026 (262 days out) with no evidence-based date behind it. Last genuine two-way buyer touch: Peter Rodwell's incoming email 9 Jun 2026 saying things are progressing slower and nothing is needed. Since then only rep-side outreach (24 Jun webinar invites, auto-reply from Neetu). Buyer explicitly requested no contact until ~November 2026 (a self-imposed 4-month pause). Owner reassigned from Adam Hasan to Luke Dougherty on 30 Jun 2026. Pace: parked at qualification with a buyer-requested pause ahead and no next milestone booked.
evidence: Buyer parked the deal: Peter Rodwell emailed 9 Jun 2026 'Things are progressing a bit slower than anticipated on our end, so nothing currently we need assistance on' and asked to reconnect over the winter period, tentatively November (source: Salesforce Task email 9 Jun 2026, Next_Step__c)

Single substantive engagement is the 23 Mar 2026 scoping call with Peter Rodwell and Raj Kiran confirming manual email/spreadsheet sourcing and contracting pain and a Phase 1 scope (iSource, iContract, iSupplier, iRisk, iAnalyze) (source: Avoma meeting 23 Mar 2026 per prior record)

No economic buyer, decision maker, or executive sponsor identified; all three mapped contacts are mid-level: Peter Rodwell (Procurement Systems Lead), Raj Kiran (Program Manager), Neetu Tickoo (Category Manager Logistics) (source: OpportunityContactRole)
No forcing function surfaced: no contract expiration, budget-approval process, fiscal deadline, or compliance driver in any field or call (source: Salesforce, scoping call 23 Mar 2026)
Close date is a placeholder reset to 25 Mar 2027 on 26 Jun 2026, not a buyer-stated decision date (source: OpportunityFieldHistory 26 Jun 2026)
Owner handed from Adam Hasan to Luke Dougherty on 30 Jun 2026, so relationship continuity with Peter needs re-establishing during the pause (source: OpportunityFieldHistory 30 Jun 2026)
No competitor named in any source across the scoping call, Next_Step__c, or Salesforce competition fields, leaving the evaluation approach unknown (source: Competitors__c null, prior record)

metrics.narrative: Pain is confirmed and operational but never quantified. On the 23 Mar 2026 scoping call Raj Kiran and Peter Rodwell described sourcing and contract activities run manually through email and spreadsheets, driving the need for an automated source-to-contract platform. When Adam Hasan asked what percentage of spend is under contract today and how much improvement they wanted, the transcript captured no numeric answer. There is no baseline (current contract coverage %, cycle time, error rate) and no target ROI or savings figure. Quantifying the value case with Peter and Raj is the immediate move needed to build an executive business case.
sources: Avoma scoping call 23 Mar 2026 (Raj Kiran, Peter Rodwell) per prior record
Salesforce: no Value_to_Customer__c / Compelling_Event__c value captured

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is identified after the full qualification cycle. The three mapped contacts are all mid-level operators: Peter Rodwell (Procurement Systems Lead), Raj Kiran (Program Manager), and Neetu Tickoo (Category Manager Logistics). No senior procurement, finance, or transformation leader has appeared on any call or email, no budget-approval process is documented, and the 23 Mar 2026 scoping call included no commercial or budget-authority discussion. What the evidence does show is that Peter controls the pace ('nothing currently we need assistance on', 9 Jun 2026), which signals he is a gatekeeper, not the budget owner. Identifying and reaching the budget owner (likely a CPO or Finance leader above Peter) is a top move for the November reconnection.
sources: OpportunityContactRole (Peter Rodwell, Raj Kiran, Neetu Tickoo, no EB role)

decision_criteria.narrative: The functional criteria are inferred from the Phase 1 scope confirmed on 23 Mar 2026: automate manual sourcing and contract management across iSource, iContract, iSupplier, iRisk, and iAnalyze, for roughly 25 power users, with Oracle EBS integration. The buyer confirmed the modules and the integration target but no formal weighted evaluation criteria, scorecard, RFP, or must-have feature list has been stated. The AIS 9 Strong signal and the Agentic AI webinar invites suggest AI capability could become a differentiating criterion, but the buyer has not yet named AI as a scored requirement. Surfacing Boral's formal selection criteria (and whether a BRD or RFP exists) is needed before shortlisting.
sources: Avoma scoping call 23 Mar 2026 per prior record

decision_process.narrative: The buying and approval process is largely unknown. The only structured event is the 23 Mar 2026 scoping call; there has been no demo, RFI/RFP, workshop, or exec review since. Peter Rodwell owns the cadence and has unilaterally paused the process to November 2026, which tells us the decision is not yet time-boxed and no formal evaluation gate is scheduled. We do not know who approves the purchase, whether multiple approvals are required, or how vendors will be compared. Mapping the decision process and approval chain with Peter is a prerequisite to forecasting a real close date.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are entirely unexplored. The 23 Mar 2026 scoping call touched system scope and approval logins inside the workflow, not MSA/SOW, legal redlines, InfoSec, or procurement channel. As a new-business full-suite S2C deal in Australia this would eventually need an MSA plus Order Form and a separately signed SOW, but none of that is in motion and no contract-expiration or compliance deadline exists to force it. The deal is far too early (Qualified) for paper to be a risk yet; visibility will come once a demo and commercials advance. No action needed here now beyond noting it as future scope.
sources: Avoma scoping call 23 Mar 2026 per prior record

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is clearly articulated and owned. Boral runs sourcing and contract activities manually via email and spreadsheets, creating inefficiency and weak process control, confirmed by Raj Kiran and Peter Rodwell on the 23 Mar 2026 scoping call. When asked what happens if they continue as-is over 12 to 24 months, the buyer acknowledged the status quo is unsustainable but did not quantify the cost. The pain owners are Peter Rodwell (Procurement Systems Lead) and Raj Kiran (Program Manager), both accountable for the process, but the pain has not been escalated to an executive sponsor or tied to a fiscal or compliance deadline, which is exactly why urgency is low and Peter felt able to defer to November.
sources: Avoma scoping call 23 Mar 2026 (Raj Kiran, Peter Rodwell) per prior record

champion.narrative: No champion exists. Peter Rodwell is the primary and most-engaged contact but is reactive, not advocating: he requested a 4-month pause and stated nothing is needed. Raj Kiran participated in the March scoping call but has not re-engaged since. Neetu Tickoo (Category Manager Logistics) expressed interest in a Zycus demo at ProcureCon 2026 and is the warmest signal, but that interest is untested and no follow-up demo has been booked. None of the three has introduced Zycus to leadership, shared internal documents, or pushed to accelerate. Developing a champion, most plausibly Neetu given her stated interest, or converting Peter from gatekeeper to advocate, is the highest-priority relationship move.
sources: OpportunityContactRole (Peter Rodwell, Raj Kiran, Neetu Tickoo)
Salesforce Task email 9 Jun 2026 (Peter Rodwell pause request)

competition.narrative: No competitor is named in any source: Competitors__c is null, the 23 Mar 2026 scoping call named no rival, and Next_Step__c and history carry no competitive references. For a full-suite $260k S2C evaluation (five modules) a competitive field is likely but currently invisible, which is a blind spot rather than confirmation of sole-source. The buyer-requested pause to November creates a window in which a rival could build relationships unseen. The move is to ask Peter directly during the reconnection whether Boral is running a competitive process and who else is in frame, so Zycus can position Merlin AI and unified S2C depth against a known field rather than in the dark.
sources: Avoma scoping call 23 Mar 2026 per prior record

deal_movement.summary: Momentum is thin and buyer-throttled. After a single 23 Mar 2026 scoping call the deal went quiet, the buyer asked on 9 Jun 2026 to pause until ~November, the record was tidied administratively in late June (advanced to Qualified, close date reset to Mar 2027, owner moved to Luke Dougherty), but no new buyer-facing progress has occurred.

items.change: Peter Rodwell requested a pause until the winter period (tentatively November 2026), citing slow internal progress and no current needs
Stage advanced Initial Interest to Qualified
Close date reset from 25 Mar 2026 to 25 Mar 2027 (placeholder, not buyer-stated)
Owner reassigned from Adam Hasan to Luke Dougherty
Rep-side re-engagement only: Agentic AI webinar invites sent to Peter, Raj and Neetu (Neetu on leave auto-reply)

competitive_position.summary: No competitor has been named in any source (Competitors__c null, the 23 Mar 2026 scoping call, and Next_Step__c all silent), so the field is a genuine blind spot rather than a confirmed sole-source. On a full-suite $260k S2C deal a competitive evaluation is likely but invisible, and the buyer-requested pause to November 2026 gives any unseen rival room to build preference. The single most important competitive action is to establish, at the reconnection, whether Boral is running a formal evaluation and who else is in it; the real current threat is buyer inertia (do-nothing / keep managing procurement on email and spreadsheets), not a named rival.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Fit on paper is strong: Boral's manual, spreadsheet-driven sourcing and contracting is exactly the pain the confirmed Phase 1 module scope (iSource, iContract, iSupplier, iRisk, iAnalyze) addresses, and AIS is 9 (Strong). The exposure is not fit, it is urgency and access: no exec sponsor, no forcing function, and a buyer who has chosen to wait.

best_practice_check.summary: Win-strategy priorities for a strong-fit deal the buyer has parked: convert warm interest into a champion, break single-threading to reach budget authority, and manufacture urgency the buyer currently lacks.
flags: Convert Neetu Tickoo's ProcureCon demo interest into a booked working session; she is the one warm signal and the most likely champion.
Break single-threading beyond Peter Rodwell: reach a senior procurement or finance leader to find the budget owner before November.
Create urgency the deal lacks: quantify the cost of Boral's manual process so the pause has a business consequence, not just a calendar slot.
Probe the evaluation approach and competitive field at reconnection to remove the current competitive blind spot.

champion_strength.summary: No champion after qualification. Peter Rodwell is the most engaged but reactive and has parked the deal; Raj Kiran has gone quiet since March; Neetu Tickoo's ProcureCon demo interest is the warmest but untested signal. None has advocated internally, opened exec access, or driven urgency. Building a champion (most plausibly Neetu) is the priority relationship move.
champion_strength.source: OpportunityContactRole, Next_Step__c, Avoma scoping call 23 Mar 2026 per prior record

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AIS is 9 (Strong) and Zycus is nurturing with Agentic AI webinar content, but the Merlin / agentic S2C story has not been put in front of Boral in a working session yet; the buyer has not engaged on AI as a criterion. The AI wedge is real but under-activated.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 9 Strong: Boral's scale, complex supplier network, and digital-transformation investment make it a strong AI-enabled procurement candidate. Buyer calls have not yet tested AI appetite directly, so the field score is not yet corroborated or contradicted by buyer statements; the 24 Jun Agentic AI webinar invite is an attempt to open that thread.

No economic buyer or exec sponsor mapped; all three contacts are mid-level, so there is no one with budget authority to accelerate or approve
Main threat is do-nothing: Boral keeps running procurement on email and spreadsheets and never prioritizes a decision, a no-decision risk rather than a competitive loss
Single-threaded on Peter Rodwell as gatekeeper; owner just changed to Luke Dougherty so relationship continuity must be re-established

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling: the last genuine two-way exchange was Peter's 9 Jun 2026 email asking to pause, followed only by rep-side outreach. The deal is not dark yet but the buyer has actively throttled it.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Boral is a strong-fit ($260k, AIS 9 Strong) full-suite S2C opportunity, but the buyer has parked it to November 2026 with no champion, no economic buyer, and no forcing function, so the one thing between us and momentum is converting Neetu Tickoo's demo interest into a sponsored evaluation before the pause hardens into a no-decision.

win_position.text: Position is early and buyer-throttled: Peter Rodwell parked the deal to November 2026 ('nothing currently we need assistance on', 9 Jun 2026) and no one with budget authority is engaged, so nothing is committed despite strong product fit.

The fit case is genuinely strong: the 23 Mar 2026 scoping call confirmed Boral's manual email/spreadsheet sourcing pain maps directly to the confirmed five-module Phase 1 scope, and AIS is 9 (Strong).
No champion and no economic buyer after qualification: all three contacts are mid-level and Peter is a gatekeeper, so there is no one advocating internally or able to approve a purchase.
The competitive field is invisible (Competitors__c null, no rival named on any call), so a rival could build preference during the pause unseen.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum has stalled to a single 23 Mar 2026 scoping call followed by a buyer-requested pause; the only recent activity is rep-side webinar invites on 24 Jun 2026, not buyer engagement.
The close date is a placeholder reset to 25 Mar 2027 on 26 Jun 2026, not a buyer-stated decision date, so there is no real timeline pulling the deal forward.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer has explicitly deferred rather than committed: Peter asked to reconnect around November 2026 and said nothing is currently needed (9 Jun 2026).
One live pull-signal remains: Neetu Tickoo expressed interest in a Zycus demo at ProcureCon 2026, the warmest commitment signal on the deal, but no demo is yet booked.

deal_risk.text: Primary risk is inertia / do-nothing: with no forcing function and a self-imposed pause, Boral may keep running procurement manually and never prioritize a decision.
Structural risk from single-threading on Peter Rodwell plus a fresh owner change to Luke Dougherty (30 Jun 2026) that must not drop the relationship during the pause.
